questions.question: What is image annotation in computer vision?

options.A: Compressing images to reduce file size
options.B: Labeling, tagging, or outlining objects or regions to create ground truth data
options.C: Uploading images directly to a website
options.D: Deleting low quality images from a dataset

questions.question: What is the main purpose of annotation data?

options.A: To make images look more colorful
options.B: To train AI and machine learning models to identify and interpret visual data
options.C: To reduce the number of images in a dataset

questions.question: Which annotation method is most accurate for complex tasks but slow and time consuming?
Which annotation method is fastest and highly scalable for straightforward datasets?
What is the best practical approach to annotation according to the material?

options.A: Use AI labeling only and never review results
options.B: Use manual labeling only for every image
options.C: Use AI labeling for initial labeling and manual review for validation
options.D: Skip labeling if the model is modern

questions.question: Which annotation type is quickest and easiest to draw?
Which annotation type is more precise than a bounding box and can slightly improve performance?
Which annotation type gives the highest accuracy for complex pixel-level boundaries?
Segmentation tasks usually require which annotation types for precise object separation?
In Roboflow, where can you access the labeling interface?

options.A: By selecting an image from the Assign or Dataset pages

questions.question: What does the Drag and Select tool allow you to do?

options.B: Select, edit, drag annotations, pan the canvas, and deselect boxes

questions.question: What is the main function of the bounding box annotation tool?
What does the polygon annotation tool draw?
What does the brush annotation tool create?
In the brush tool, what do Add and Subtract modes do?

options.B: Add to or erase from the current mask

questions.question: What is Smart Polygon used for?

options.A: Drawing Smart Polygon annotations that can be refined

questions.question: In Smart Polygon, what does a green dot indicate?

options.B: Adding a new area of interest

questions.question: In Smart Polygon, what does a red dot indicate?

options.A: Removing parts from the selected area

questions.question: Which settings can refine Smart Polygon output?
What does Label Assist do?

options.A: Automatically suggests bounding box labels using model predictions
options.D: Deploys models to a private cloud server

questions.question: What is Mark Null used for?

options.B: Labeling background/null images or clearing annotations

questions.question: Which Roboflow training option is ideal for production-ready models?
Which Roboflow training option trains models in a few minutes and is ideal for testing?
What does Neural Architecture Search do?

options.A: Discovers novel model architectures and fine tunes at the same time
options.C: Streams webcam video to the cloud

questions.question: What must you generate first before training a computer vision model in Roboflow?
Which button starts configuration for a training job?
What is a model architecture?

options.A: The machine learning technology used to train the model
options.B: The folder where images are stored

questions.question: For object detection, which architecture is described as offering the best accuracy?
For instance segmentation, which architecture is described as offering the best accuracy?
Which model sizes are listed for RF-DETR?
What does training from a checkpoint mean in context?

options.A: Starting training from a selected previously learned starting point

questions.question: What happens after clicking Start Training?

options.A: The dataset is zipped and prepared for training in the Roboflow cloud
options.C: The model is immediately deployed to CCTV

questions.question: What does the training summary show before training starts?
Which loss measures how accurately the model locates objects?
Which loss measures how correctly the model identifies object classes?
Which loss measures whether the model reliably detects object presence?
For box, class, and objectness losses, what generally indicates better training progress?
What should you do first after training a model?
What does mAP measure?

options.A: The performance of computer vision models

questions.question: Mean Average Precision is the average of what metric across all classes?
What does precision focus on avoiding?
What does recall focus on avoiding?
Where can you quickly test a model on an image in Roboflow?
Why is Visualize useful before building application logic in Workflows?

options.A: It runs quick checks on model performance
options.D: It removes the need for testing

questions.question: Which deployment method is ideal when the deployment device has a persistent internet connection?
What can the Serverless Hosted API be used with?

options.A: Workflows, REST API, and Inference Python SDK

questions.question: What is the file upload limit mentioned for the Serverless Hosted API?
What technology does the Serverless Video Streaming API use?
Which input sources are supported by the Serverless Video Streaming API?

options.A: Webcam, RTSP streams, and video files

questions.question: What is RTSP described as?

options.A: A way to access a live video stream from a camera over the internet or a local network

questions.question: What are Dedicated Deployments?

options.A: Private cloud servers managed by Roboflow for running computer vision models

questions.question: Which model/application types can Dedicated Deployments run?

options.A: Object detection, segmentation, classification, keypoint detection, foundation models, and Workflows

questions.question: What is Batch Processing best for?

options.A: Asynchronously processing large amounts of images or stored videos
options.B: Manually drawing one box at a time

questions.question: Where do you start creating a Batch Processing job?

options.A: Deployments sidebar, then Batch Processing tab

questions.question: What must you select first when configuring a batch processing job?
Matching/Identification: The confusion-matrix result when the model predicts an object and the object is actually present.
Matching/Identification: The confusion-matrix result when the model predicts an object but the object is not actually present.
Matching/Identification: The confusion-matrix result when the model predicts no object and no object is actually present.
Matching/Identification: The confusion-matrix result when the model predicts no object but the object is actually present.
The model predicts a dog, and there is actually a dog in the image.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The model predicts a cat, but there is actually no cat in the image.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The model says there is no car, and there is indeed no car in the image.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The model says there is no person, but there is actually a person in the image.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: A medical test predicts a disease, and the patient actually has the disease.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The model detects a fire alarm when there is actually no fire in the scene.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The system correctly identifies that no intruder is present in a security feed.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: A medical model predicts no disease, but the patient actually has the disease.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The system correctly flags a spam email as spam.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: The system labels a normal email as spam.
questions.hint: Answer with TP, FP, TN, or FN. TP = predicted present and actually present; FP = predicted present but absent; TN = predicted absent and actually absent; FN = predicted absent but actually present.

questions.question: Identification: The metric that asks, of all predicted objects, how many were actually correct.
Identification: The metric that asks, of all real objects, how many were successfully detected.
Identification: The metric equal to the average of Average Precision across all classes.
Identification: The annotation tool used to paint pixel-precise masks.
Identification: The Roboflow feature that suggests labels using model predictions.
Identification: The deployment option for asynchronous large-batch image or stored-video processing.
Explain why combining AI labeling with manual validation is often better than using only one annotation method.
expectedConcepts: good for initial labels then review
questions.hint: Hint: Compare speed/scalability with accuracy/context checking.

questions.question: Compare bounding boxes, polygons, and masks. Explain when each annotation type is most appropriate.
questions.hint: Hint: Order them from fastest/easiest to most precise/pixel-level.

questions.question: A model has high precision but low recall. Explain what this means and what kind of error the model is probably still making often.
questions.hint: Hint: Precision is about predicted objects being correct; recall is about finding all real objects.

questions.question: Describe the major steps for training a Roboflow model from dataset preparation to starting the training job.
questions.hint: Hint: Start with dataset version, then Custom Train settings, then Start Training.

questions.question: Explain why viewing metrics after training is necessary before deployment.
questions.hint: Hint: Mention mAP, precision, recall, and finding weaknesses before production.

questions.question: Choose between Serverless Hosted API, Serverless Video Streaming API, Dedicated Deployment, and Batch Processing for a real use case. Justify your choice.
questions.hint: Hint: Match the deployment option to internet availability, live video, privacy, or large asynchronous batches.

questions.question: Explain the difference between box loss, class loss, and objectness loss during object detection training.
questions.hint: Hint: Think location, category, and object presence.

questions.question: A security camera model misses a person in a restricted area. Identify the confusion-matrix result and explain why it is dangerous.
questions.hint: Hint: The model says no object/person, but the object/person is actually present.

questions.question: Explain how Visualize, Workflows, and deployment connect in a practical Roboflow project.
questions.hint: Hint: Visualize checks the model, Workflows build logic, deployment runs it in real use.

questions.question: Give a practical study explanation of the complete Roboflow model lifecycle covered in the finals document.
questions.hint: Hint: Walk through annotation, dataset versioning, training, metrics, testing, and deployment.
